headline.rest: Then spend years — and six figures — paying tax authorities, banks and lawyers to undo it.
sub: We recommend the right fit across 79 jurisdictions, not the one that pays us most.
globe.title.part2: where you live both decide whether Stripe, Mercury, and Wise will onboard you.
globe.support: Mercury and Wise gate on your country of residence; Stripe on the company's country. Miss either list and you're locked out.
chart.title: A zero-tax offshore company can still be taxed at home — CFC rules and treaty gaps quietly erode every cross-border dollar.
chart.support: Tax follows the people who control the company, not the registry.
burst.title: VCs read a jurisdiction mismatch as amateur execution, and a non-Delaware entity can stall the round mid-diligence.
burst.support: Most US venture money requires a Delaware C-corp.
holo.label: Certificate of Incorporation
holo.title: Moving a mis-incorporated entity is a full cross-border reorganization — legal and accounting fees routinely run five figures.
holo.support: The flip triggers tax advice in two countries and fresh consents.
substance.title: Offshore hubs now demand real economic substance — and one grey- or blacklisting can wreck your banking and tax position overnight.
substance.support: Lists change after you incorporate, often with little warning.
table.title: One missed filing can dissolve the entity holding your IP, bank accounts and cap table.
table.support: Lost good standing surfaces at the worst moment — mid-raise.
table.rows.holdco.name: HoldCo
table.rows.topco.name: TopCo
table.rows.opco.name: OpCo
table.rows.ipco.name: IP Co
table.rows.subco.name: SubCo
